mission.title: dapr: Updated python script to find latest release version and set env var
mission.description:
# Description

This PR modifies one of the python scripts used in the Github Actions Workflows to fix the issue mentioned in https://github.com/dapr/dapr/issues/5625. It defines a boolean function that checks the `.md` file names in `docs/release_notes` to find semantic versions (minus `-rc` release candidates) and compares the highest version against the release version being inputted to the script.

This was required as the script currently was not actually checking whether the release was the latest version, and instead just validating that the provided version had a release note against it and that it wasn't an `-rc` release. This introduces issues with the `:latest` image tag when a patch for a previous major version (e.g., `1.9.*`) is released.

## Issue reference

Please reference the issue this PR will close: #5625

## Checklist

I tested the code locally but there are no unit / end-to-end tests that exist, since it is just a workflow script.

Please make sure you've  completed
